items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>As President Donald Trump tours wildfire damage in California, he's blaming the state's water policies for worsening the fires that have devastated the Los Angeles area. But water experts don't see a connection between the two. Read more: https://bit.ly/4aO1uXn

items.content:

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Former Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio said at a Friday news conference in Florida that he did "nothing wrong" around the January 6, 2021 riot at the US Capitol. Tarrio's 22-year sentence on seditious conspiracy charges was pardoned by President Trump this week. (AP Video: Cody Jackson).

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Pete Hegseth, President Donald Trump’s nominee for defense secretary, paid $50,000 to the woman who accused him of sexual assault in 2017, according to answers he provided to a senator during his confirmation process that The Associated Press has obtained.

items.title: A$AP Rocky pushes past photographers to enter court
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Rapper A$AP Rocky pushed past several photographers on Friday as he entered a Los Angeles court for opening statements in his assault trial.

Rocky, whose legal name is Rakim Mayers, is accused of shooting a former friend near a Hollywood hotel in 2021.

#shorts #asaprocky #trial #losangeles</article>

items.title: Putin says he is ready for talks with Trump: He's 'clever and 'pragmatic'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Russian President Vladimir Putin on Friday seconded U.S. President Donald Trump's claim that he could have prevented the conflict in Ukraine if he were in office and said that Moscow is ready for talks with the U.S. on a broad range of issues. Read more: https://bit.ly/4hrj5Gw

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Watch live from Asheville as President Donald Trump and first lady Melania Trump travel to western North Carolina to meet families affected by Hurricane Helene.

Trump is using the first trip of his second administration to visit areas where politics has complicated the response to deadly disasters. Follow live updates here: https://bit.ly/3Ck642E

#trump #news #live #donaldtrump</article>

items.content:

items.title: Indonesia and France sign deal to send home ailing French national on death row for drug offenses
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Serge Atlaoui will be repatriated to France after being on death row since 2007 for alleged drug offenses. The 61-year-old won a last-minute reprieve from being executed by a 13-member firing squad In 2015.
(AP Video by Fadlan Syam)

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The executive order Trump signed Thursday also aims to declassify the remaining federal records relating to the assassinations of President John F. Kennedy, Sen. Robert F. Kennedy and Rev. Martin Luther King Jr. The order is among a flurry of executive actions Trump has quickly taken the first week of his second term. (Produced by Luke Garratt). Read more: https://bit.ly/40Nj0Hu

items.title: Belarus election is poised to extend the 30-year rule of 'Europe's last dictator'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>With many of his political opponents either jailed or exiled abroad, 70-year-old Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko is back on the ballot. When the election concludes on Sunday, he's all but certain to add a seventh term as the only leader most people in post-Soviet Belarus have ever known. Read more: https://bit.ly/4jxrudd 

#belarus #news 
Photo credit: Belarus' Presidential Press Service

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Live from the Smithsonian National Zoo: a panda ceremony with zoo staff leaders, as giant pandas Bao Li and Qing Bao make their public debut. The furry black-and-white icons are on a 10-year loan from China, which owns all the pandas in the world.

items.content:
<article>Watch live as the Senate is muscling Pete Hegseth’s nomination as defense secretary toward confirmation Friday.

With votes expected late in the evening, the Republican-led Senate is determined to install Hegseth, a former Fox News host and combat veteran, and round out President Donald Trump’s top national security Cabinet officials. Read more: https://bit.ly/3C9Rqeo

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

items.title: Israeli families call on US President Trump to ensure all hostages held in Gaza released
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Families of Israeli hostages have called on US President Donald Trump to ensure all hostages held in Gaza are released. Among those appealing to Trump was Simcha Goldin, the father of Hadar Goldin, an Israeli soldier whose body has been held by militants in Gaza since 2014 despite a public campaign by the family to return his remains. (AP video shot by Shlomo Mor)

items.title: Aftermath of overnight Russian drone attack in Ukraine's Kyiv region
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>At least 3 people have died following a Russian overnight drone attack in Ukraine's Kyiv region on Friday. According to State Emergency Service of Ukraine, drone fragments hit a ten-story apartment building in Fastiv district, causing fire and destruction on several floors. One more person was injured and taken to hospital, Ukrainian National Police said.

#russia #ukraine #news

items.content:

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Karim Khan said the court is waiting to "see if another executive order is signed or if there are other bills passed by Congress." items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

The hand and nose of the statue of Cook, who charted Sydney’s coast in 1770, were severed and the sandstone statue was doused with red paint.

items.content:

items.content:

To fishermen, the invasive plant is a threat to livelihoods. But a local company is working with local fishermen to collect water hyacinth and turn it into biodegradable packaging. But experts say scaling up such work will be a challenge. Read more: https://bit.ly/4hrqGoA

items.title: Search resumes after deadly flooding and landslides in Indonesia
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Rescuers have retrieved two more bodies as they resumed their search for people missing after floods and landslides on Indonesia’s main island of Java, bringing the death toll to 19. Waters from flooded rivers tore through nine villages in Pekalongan regency of Central Java province and landslides tumbled onto mountainside hamlets after the torrential rains. Read more: https://bit.ly/3PJX3CX

#flood #landslide #indonesia #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Witness says people leaped out of burning hotel in Turkey; at least 75 dead
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A worker from a hotel nearby the popular Turkish ski resort where at least 75 people were killed by a raging fire recounted a harrowing experience of death and despair. The blaze through the 12-storey hotel in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday also injured at least 51 people. (AP video: Mehmet Guzel). Read more: https://bit.ly/3EfyECF

#skiresorts #fire #turkey #news
